text.title: django-pytest-fixtures Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for django-pytest-fixtures as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: db vs transactional_db confusion: using db for a test that asserts on commit semantics, post-commit hooks, or signals.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The db fixture wraps the test in a savepoint; post-commit hooks and signals that fire on real commits never execute. Use transactional_db for any test involving transaction.on_commit, signals, post_save, or select_for_update.

failure-mode.description: Session-scoped mutable fixtures: creating a session-scoped factory instance that different tests modify.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Session-scoped fixtures are shared across all tests in the session. Any mutation accumulates and creates order-dependent failures. Session scope is only safe for truly immutable data (read-only reference tables, static config).

failure-mode.description: pytest-xdist + --reuse-db racing on migrations on first run.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Multiple workers try to create the database simultaneously. Use --create-db on cold start (first run or after schema changes), then --reuse-db for subsequent runs.

failure-mode.description: override_settings inside a fixture without using the pytest-django settings fixture.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: override_settings as a context manager or decorator does not cascade cleanly into session-scoped fixtures. Use the settings fixture from pytest-django; it is properly scoped and cleaned up.

failure-mode.description: MEDIA_ROOT not overridden in test settings, causing tests to write files to the real disk.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Tests that create uploads pollute the real MEDIA_ROOT and each other. Always override MEDIA_ROOT via an autouse fixture using tmp_path so each test gets a clean directory that is deleted after the test.
